methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Navier is a seed-stage transportation company that has raised $7.2M to date. The transportation sector comp universe consists of five private peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables available. A narrowly-filtered peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within transportation; only one adjacent-stage peer could be identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability.

Consequently, the valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression to the $7.2M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $18M. This method assumes that capital raised serves as a reasonable baseline for seed-stage value creation and that historical step-up patterns hold for this company.

The triangulated range is $15M to $21M, centered at $18M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier for Navier's specific market, the absence of direct peer valuation data to validate the stage-progression assumption, and the nascent state of comparable companies in this subsector, which limits confidence in cross-sectional benchmarking. A subsequent funding round with disclosed terms or additional peer data would significantly narrow this range.
